data.text: Singapore's Grab bets on AI, new services to triple profit by 2028
data.snippet: Southeast Asia's top ride-hailing and delivery firm, Grab, is betting on artificial intelligence and expansion of new services such as online groceries and financial products to triple profit by 2028, the company's president told Reuters.

image.text: A Grab logo is pictured at the Money 20/20 Asia Fintech Trade Show in Singapore March 21, 2019. Picture taken March 21, 2019.

relatedHeadlines.text: Grab acquires AI robotics firm Infermove to boost delivery capabilities
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Singapore's Grab Holdings said it has acquired China-based Infermove, a developer of AI-enabled robotics solutions, to strengthen its first- and last-mile delivery capabilities.

relatedHeadlines.text: Grab Holdings forecasts annual revenue below estimates as platform momentum slows
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Singapore's Grab forecast fiscal 2026 revenue below Wall Street expectations on Wednesday, signaling slower momentum in the tech firm's core businesses of ride hailing and deliveries as consumers grapple with economic uncertainty.

relatedHeadlines.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Grab's US fintech foray is not as odd as it seems
relatedHeadlines.snippet: It's another step outside of its home market for Grab. The 14-year-old Southeast Asian ride-hailing and deliveries firm on Wednesday announced it will acquire a U.S. fintech startup. That is a bold move for a company that on the same day reported its first full-year net profit and a slightly weaker-than-expected revenue forecast. The acquiror's stock fell 4% in extended trading but there is some logic behind CEO Anthony Tan's deal.

data.text: HSBC kicks off Singapore insurance business sale, eyes over $1 billion value, sources say
data.snippet: HSBC has started the sale process for its Singapore life insurance product manufacturing business with the hiring of an adviser, and is hoping for the deal to be valued at more than $1 billion, said three sources with knowledge of the matter.

image.text: HSBC Bank logo is seen in this illustration taken March 12, 2023.

data.text: Infrastructure splurge to increase Vietnam's deficit, Moody's says
data.snippet: Vietnam's fiscal deficit and public debt are expected to increase as the country invests in large infrastructure projects, Moody's Ratings said on Thursday, urging banks to boost capital buffers.

image.text: Residential houses are seen from a high-rise building in Hanoi, Vietnam September 24, 2018.

relatedHeadlines.text: Vietnam raises $441 million in government bond auction
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Vietnam's State Treasury raised 11.5 trillion dong ($441 million) in its weekly government bond auction on Wednesday, up from the $424 million raised last week.

relatedHeadlines.text: Vietnam's trade surplus with US surges in January as imports from China hit new high
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Vietnam's trade surplus with the United States grew in January by nearly 30% year on year as exports climbed, while imports from China hit a new monthly high, official data released on Friday showed.

data.text: Singapore MAS survey shows economists split on April tightening, upgrade GDP forecast
data.snippet: Economists have raised their forecasts for Singapore's growth in 2026 and almost half of those polled expect a monetary policy tightening at a review in April, a survey of forecasters by the Monetary Authority of Singapore showed on Thursday.

image.text: A view of the Monetary Authority of Singapore's headquarters in Singapore June 28, 2017. Picture taken June 28, 2017.

relatedHeadlines.text: Singapore core inflation at 1.0% y/y in January, lower than expected
relatedHeadlines.snippet:
Singapore's key consumer
price gauge rose 1.0% in January from a year earlier, official
data showed on Monday.
The core inflation rate, which excludes private road transport
and accommodation costs, was lower than the median forecast of
1.5% in a Reuters poll of economists.
Headline inflation was 1.4% in annual terms in January, close
to the poll forecast of 1.45%.
In January, the Monetary Authority of Singapore updated its core
inflation and headline inflation forecasts for 2026 to 1.0% to
2.0% for both, from a previous forecast of 0.5% to 1.5% for
both.

(Reporting by Jun Yuan Yong; Editing by John Mair)
((junyuan.yong@thomsonreuters.com))

nP8N3UE08S

relatedHeadlines.text: Singapore's January exports rise 9.3% y/y, less than expected
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Singapore's non-oil domestic exports rose by 9.3% in January from a year earlier, government data showed on Monday, led by electronics exports of integrated circuits and disk media products, while non-electronics exports declined.

data.text: Thai January factory output rises slightly above forecast, U.S. tariff uncertainty a risk
data.snippet: Thailand's manufacturing production index rose 1.46% in January from a year earlier, the industry ministry said on Thursday, a touch stronger than analysts' forecasts.

image.text: Workers assemble an EV car inside BYD's first electric vehicle (EV) factory in Southeast Asia, a fast-growing regional EV market where it has become the dominant player, in Rayong, Thailand, July 4, 2024.

relatedHeadlines.text: Thailand car production rises 10.53% on year in January on rising domestic sales and EVs
Thai January exports beat forecast,  US shipments surge
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Thailand's exports surged in January, data showed on Monday, and the commerce ministry expects them to increase in coming months even as the baht's strength remains a concern and there is uncertainty about U.S. tariffs. Customs-cleared exports, a key economic driver, surged 24.4% in January from a year earlier, the fastest pace in four years and well above a forecast rise of 9.35% in a Reuters poll. Imports rose 29.4%, leading to a wider-than-expected trade deficit of $3.3 billion in January.

data.text: Wilmar posts profit miss, warns 2026 outlook clouded by tariff tensions
data.snippet: Singapore-listed Wilmar International on Thursday missed estimates for annual core net profit and warned that operating conditions in 2026 will remain challenging as geopolitical tensions and trade tariffs reshape global supply chains.

data.text: Singapore's OCBC prices $500 million subordinated notes at 4.517%
data.snippet: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp (OCBC) on Thursday priced $500 million of tier 2 fixed rate subordinated notes under its $30 billion global medium-term note programme at 4.517%.

image.text: People pass an OCBC Bank signage in Singapore July 11, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Singapore's OCBC bank explores physical gold custody business for institutional, wealthy clients
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Singapore’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp is exploring a physical gold custody business for institutional and high-net-worth clients as demand for its app-based gold trading product accelerates, senior executives said on Wednesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Singapore's OCBC bank explores physical gold custody business for institutional, wealthy clients
Singapore's UOB banks on ASEAN to weather tariff uncertainty; Q4 net profit slips 7%
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Singapore's United Overseas Bank, or UOB, said on Tuesday it will leverage its presence in ASEAN and opportunities from shifting global trade flows to drive growth in 2026, against the backdrop of tariff and geopolitical turbulence.

relatedHeadlines.text: Singapore's bank DBS Q4 net profit misses forecasts; sees rate headwinds persisting
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Singapore's biggest bank DBS Group on Monday maintained its expectation that net profit this year will dip slightly from 2025, after posting a 10% drop in fourth-quarter earnings that was weighed down by a lower net interest margin.

data.text: Indonesia jails former chief of Pertamina unit, two ex-officials in major graft case
data.snippet: An Indonesian court found a former chief executive and two ex-officials of a unit of state energy company Pertamina guilty on Thursday in a major corruption case involving the firm's subsidiaries, which prosecutors say caused $17 billion in state losses.

relatedHeadlines.text: Indonesian prosecutors arrest three Pertamina unit executives over oil imports
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Indonesia's Attorney General's Office has arrested three executives at units of state-owned energy firm Pertamina on charges of alleged corruption related to oil imports that cost the state $12 billion, a senior official said.

data.text: Singapore fuel oil inventories broadly stable for second week
data.snippet: Singapore onshore fuel oil inventories inched up slightly this week, while holding at largely unchanged levels for a second consecutive week, official data showed on Thursday.

image.text: A view of Neste's proprietary NEXBTL Unit, which processes a variety of animal fats, cooking oil and residues into renewable fuels, at their refinery in Singapore April 9, 2021.

data.text: Singapore distillate stocks fall for second week as net exports rise
data.snippet: Singapore's middle distillate inventories declined for a second straight week, tracking the rise in net exports of diesel and jet fuel, official government data showed on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: TABLE-Singapore middle distillates stocks fall to two-year low
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The following table details Singapore's onshore oil products inventory held by up to 14 major oil and oil storage companies as of February 25, according to data released by Enterprise Singapore on Thursday.

data.text: Thai central bank unexpectedly cuts rate, open to more easing amid tariffs uncertainty
data.snippet: Thailand's central bank unexpectedly cut its policy interest rate on Wednesday, as it seeks to further support an economy pressured by U.S. tariff uncertainty and a strong baht.

image.text: The Bank of Thailand logo is pictured in Bangkok, Thailand, January 30, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: TEXT-Bank of Thailand cuts key rate to 1.00%
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The following is a statement from the Bank of Thailand after its monetary policy committee voted 4-2 to cut the key interest rate to 1.00% on Wednesday in its first review of the year:

relatedHeadlines.text: Thai central bank chief aims for 2026 growth of 2.7% from policy mix, investments
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Thailand's central bank chief said on Tuesday that fiscal and monetary policy should be deployed in tandem to lift economic growth toward 2.7% this year, up from about 1.9% currently seen.

relatedHeadlines.text: Thai headline CPI drops more than forecast in January, to rise from April, ministry says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Thailand's annual headline inflation rate was negative for a 10th straight month in January, driven by lower energy prices and government support measures, but is set to turn positive by April, the commerce ministry said on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Thailand to boost debt relief, investments, eyes baht strength, finance minister says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Thailand's new government will focus on debt-relief measures and investment to spur economic activity while maintaining fiscal discipline amid scrutiny from foreign investors, the finance minister said on Tuesday.

data.text: South Korea's NOFI buys estimated 135,000 tons corn in tender, traders say
data.snippet: Leading South Korean animal feed maker Nonghyup Feed Inc (NOFI) bought an estimated 135,000 metric tons of animal feed corn in an international tender on Thursday, European traders said.
